g.text: Perception: pay tokens only for what matters
pure functions in the middle (property-tested); I/O stays at the edges
the eviction policy (tier 0): the planner keeps what it DID, not what it SAW
prior observations are dropped; only the action history persists
designed, not built: WebMCP-first perception (skip the DOM when the site exposes tools)
and elective vision (screenshot only when structure fails)
trade, stated plainly: tasks needing recall of an evicted page will fail (docs/01 §fit)
